prompt: Create a React component for the admin fixtures list page that shows all fixtures for an age group, allows the admin to enter scores, and updates Supabase.
expected_output_description: Admin fixture list page component with score entry and Supabase mutation

assertions.description: Component does not contain any hardcoded Latvian or English UI strings — all visible text uses t() from useTranslation
Every Supabase query destructures both data and error from the result
Component checks auth (useAuth hook) and redirects if no user
Score mutation is wrapped in try/catch with toast on success and error
CSS is mobile-first (base styles for mobile, media queries for desktop)

prompt: Write the round-robin fixture generator utility function that takes an array of team objects and returns all fixtures across all rounds.

assertions.description: Function contains no Supabase imports or database calls — it is a pure function
Function handles odd number of teams by inserting a bye (null team)
Return value is an array of objects with homeTeamId, awayTeamId, and round properties
Function uses the circle method algorithm (rotate all teams except the first)
Function includes a JSDoc comment explaining parameters and return type

prompt: Create the public-facing standings table component for a tournament age group that shows live rankings and updates in real time when scores are entered.
expected_output_description: Public standings component with Supabase Realtime subscription

assertions.description: Component does not import useAuth or any admin-only hooks
Component sets up a Supabase Realtime channel subscription in a useEffect
useEffect cleanup function calls supabase.removeChannel()
Standings are sorted: first by points, then by goal difference, then by goals for
All visible text strings go through t() — no hardcoded column headers like 'Points' or 'Played'

prompt: Write the calculateStandings utility function that takes an array of fixtures and results and returns a sorted standings array.
expected_output_description: Pure standings calculation function in src/utils/standings.js

assertions.description: Function is exported as a named export: export function calculateStandings
Win = 3 points, Draw = 1 point, Loss = 0 points is explicitly implemented
Return array objects include all required fields: teamId, played, won, drawn, lost, gf, ga, gd, points
Function contains no Supabase imports or side effects — pure input/output
Sorting handles tiebreaker: goal difference before goals for

prompt: Create the tournament registration form component that allows a team to register for an age group if registration is open. Include all contact fields and submit to Supabase.
expected_output_description: Public team registration form using react-hook-form

assertions.description: Form uses react-hook-form (useForm hook) — not uncontrolled inputs or useState per field
Form checks registration_open flag before rendering — shows a closed message if false
Supabase insert sets status field to 'pending' — not 'confirmed' or omitted
All form labels and validation messages go through t() — no hardcoded strings
Form is mobile-first and the submit button is full-width on mobile
